name: OmegaConf (Hierarchical Configuration for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Omry Yadan / Meta AI

performance.notes: Structured config access; fast in-memory; YAML loading adds I/O overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Hierarchical config with YAML, dataclass, dict backends. from omegaconf import OmegaConf, DictConfig. cfg = OmegaConf.load('config.yaml'). cfg = OmegaConf.structured(MyConfig). OmegaConf.merge(base_cfg, override_cfg). cfg.model.layers. cfg.get('key', 'default'). ${interpolation} syntax in YAML. Struct mode: cfg = OmegaConf.create({'a': 1}); OmegaConf.set_struct(cfg, True). Used by Hydra (Facebook AI). Environment variable interpolation: ${oc.env:HOME}. Dataclass integration: @dataclass class Config.

gotchas: Interpolation syntax (${key}) can conflict with shell/f-string usage
Struct mode prevents adding unexpected keys — can be confusing
DictConfig object is not a plain dict — some dict operations don't work
Primarily designed to work with Hydra — standalone use can feel over-engineered
best_when: Hierarchical config with interpolation and merge semantics
Research codebases needing config composition and override
avoid_when: Production app config without ML/research complexity
When Hydra overhead is not justified
summary: OmegaConf provides hierarchical YAML/dataclass configuration with interpolation and merge. OmegaConf.load('config.yaml'). OmegaConf.merge(base, override). ${interpolation} in YAML. Struct mode for schema enforcement. Dataclass integration. BSD-3 by Meta AI. Primary use case is powering Hydra for ML experiment config. For production web apps prefer pydantic-settings; for ML research experiments use with Hydra.
